reasoning: The Lean theorem universally quantifies over all finite-dimensional real normed spaces E of dimension m and asserts the closed unit ball is compact. This is logically equivalent to the informal claim "for any norm on ℝ^m, the closed unit ball is compact": every m-dimensional real normed space is linearly isometric to ℝ^m equipped with some norm (by transporting the norm along any linear isomorphism), so quantifying over all such E is the same as quantifying over all norms on ℝ^m. The Metric.closedBall (0 : E) 1 precisely encodes {x : ‖x‖ ≤ 1}, and the finrank = m hypothesis pins the dimension. No extra hypotheses are smuggled in and nothing essential is dropped.
